questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The COP of a refrigerator is:
questions.solution: COP_R = Q_L/W. COP_HP = Q_H/W. For Carnot: COP_R = T_L/(T_H-T_L).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine cycle is the ideal cycle for a:
questions.solution: The Rankine cycle (boiler–turbine–condenser–pump) models vapour power plants.

questions.stem: Work-energy theorem: net work done equals:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Strain energy:
options: U = ½·σ·ε per unit volume. U_total = ∫(σ²/2E)dV
questions.solution: Resilience: U at yield. Toughness: total area under σ-ε curve.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Stefan–Boltzmann law states that radiated energy is proportional to:
options: The fourth power of absolute temperature
questions.solution: E = σT⁴ for a black body; radiation rises steeply with temperature.

questions.stem: A cotter joint is used to connect:
questions.solution: Cotter joint: connects two rods axially (tension).

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Mean effective pressure is:
questions.solution: MEP = W_net/V_d. Higher MEP = more power per liter.

questions.stem: A Carnot engine works between 900 K and 400 K. Its efficiency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Casting — riser design:
options: Riser solidifies after casting (provides feed metal). Riser volume > casting shrinkage
questions.solution: Riser: location near heaviest section. Chvorinov's rule: t ∝ (V/A)². Blind riser: more efficient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annealing involves:
options: Heating then slow cooling (softens, relieves stresses)
questions.solution: Annealing: furnace cool → soft, ductile. Normalizing: air cool → medium properties. Quenching: fast cool → hard.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The involute profile of gear teeth ensures:
options: Constant velocity ratio even with slight center distance variation
questions.solution: Involute: constant angular velocity ratio regardless of center distance (within limits).

questions.solution: Leaf spring: max bending stress at centre.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Air-fuel ratio (AFR) for stoichiometric gasoline:
questions.solution: Stoichiometric AFR: gasoline ≈ 14.7, diesel ≈ 14.5. Lean > stoichiometric, rich < stoichiometric.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Brayton cycle:
options: Gas turbine. Compressor → combustor → turbine. η = 1 - 1/r_p^((γ-1)/γ)
questions.solution: Brayton: constant pressure combustion. r_p = pressure ratio. Regeneration, intercooling, reheat.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Taylor: n=0.3, tool life 30 min at 90 m/min. Tool life (min) at 70 m/min:

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Tempering of martensite:
questions.solution: Tempering: reheat 200-600C. Trades hardness for toughness.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Degrees of freedom of a spatial mechanism:
questions.solution: Spatial: each joint removes different DOF depending on type.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Additive manufacturing — 3D printing:
options: FDM (filament), SLS (powder), SLA (resin), DMLS (metal powder bed)
questions.solution: FDM: thermoplastic extrusion. SLS: laser sintering. SLA: UV curing. DMLS: metal 3D printing.

questions.stem: Machining operations:
questions.solution: Turning (lathe), milling (horizontal/vertical), drilling (drill press), grinding (abrasive wheel).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simple truss with apex load 20 kN and span 4m. The force (kN) in one principal rafter (θ=30°) is:
questions.solution: Member force = 26.7 kN (by method of joints)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Muff coupling is used to connect:
questions.solution: Muff (sleeve) coupling: connects two shafts coaxially.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Radiation shield reduces heat transfer by:
questions.solution: Radiation shield: additional surface with e < 1. Adds resistance in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a simple gear train, the intermediate gear (idler) affects:
questions.solution: Idler changes direction only; speed ratio unchanged.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Mechanical advantage of a machine:
options: Ratio of output force to input force
questions.solution: MA = F_out/F_in. Ideal MA = velocity ratio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermal diffusivity α is:
options: k/(ρCp) (ratio of thermal conductivity to thermal capacity)
questions.solution: α = k/ρCp. High α = fast thermal response (heat spreads quickly).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A flywheel stores:
options: Kinetic energy (smooths out speed fluctuations during a cycle)
questions.solution: Flywheel: Iω²/2 = stored KE. Coefficient of fluctuation = (ω_max - ω_min)/ω_mean.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A solid shaft of diameter 20 mm transmits torque T. The shear stress is maximum at:
questions.solution: Shear stress in torsion: τ = Tr/J, maximum at outer surface (r = R).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a gear pair, the velocity ratio is:
options: Inversely proportional to the number of teeth (N₁/N₂ = ω₂/ω₁)
questions.solution: ω₁/ω₂ = N₂/N₁ = d₂/d₁ = r₂/r₁ (for standard gears).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Psychrometry — dry bulb temperature:
options: Temperature measured by ordinary thermometer. Air temperature
questions.solution: DBT: actual air temperature. WBT: adiabatic saturation temperature. DPT: condensation temperature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a Rankine cycle, steam is condensed:
questions.solution: Rankine: 1-2 isentropic pump, 2-3 constant pressure boiler, 3-4 isentropic turbine, 4-1 constant pressure condenser.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which comparison of specific heats is correct for a gas?
questions.solution: At constant pressure some heat does expansion work, so Cp > Cv (Cp − Cv = R for an ideal gas).

questions.stem: The first law of thermodynamics is essentially a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: ΔU = Q − W; energy is conserved as it changes between heat and work.

questions.stem: The velocity ratio of a simple gear train depends on the:
options: Number of teeth on the gears
questions.solution: Speed ratio = N_driver/N_driven = (teeth on driven)/(teeth on driver).

questions.solution: Dh = 4A/P. For circular pipe: Dh = D.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The endurance (fatigue) limit of a material is the stress below which it:
options: Can endure virtually infinite load cycles
questions.solution: Below the endurance limit, ferrous materials survive an effectively unlimited number of cycles.
